title: Aporum tetralobum
intro: Aporum tetralobum, also known as the Four-lobed Dendrobium, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aporum tetralobum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Aporum tetralobum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to its characteristics, such as having a flower spike and modified leaves that function as petals. The genus Aporum itself is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the orchid family.
